attacking_style: Operates exclusively from early breakaways, making decisive moves inside final 25km when breakaway splinters. Favors gradual climbs and time-trialing finishes over explosive summit finishes.
team_tactics: Given minimal support — frequently in breakaways where teammates secure KOM points first (KOZNERFALL example), then allowed to target stage win. Self-reliant finishing approach.
terrain_preference: Gradual, time-trial-suited climbs where power advantage overcomes pure climbers. Excels on Pyrenean-style stages. Vulnerable on explosive, steep finishes where lighter climbers dominate.
vulnerability: Mechanical incidents when isolated in small groups without immediate team car access. Requires breakaway success — no observed strength in reduced bunch sprints or GC group tactics.
key_patterns: Won from early breakaways of 13-18 riders in all observed victories (3 instances)
Makes winning move in final 25km as breakaway fragments, not initial attack
Targets KOM points in non-GC stages, suggests season preparation racing (2 instances)
Large rider from French Alps uses power/TT ability over pure climbing (multiple references)
Thrives when teammates secure secondary objectives first, enabling stage focus (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 appearance shows KOM hunting without win threat, suggesting domestique role or form-finding after 2020 breakthrough victories
